description:
Unusual document containing meeting minutes relating to the business dealings of someone whose name is too faded to read (r2). In Judaeo-Arabic. Dating: probably 13th or 14th century. The handwriting is distinctive and may be known. 
 
(1) Mentions Muḥammad, al-Rav, and collecting and numbering and registering "copies." 
(2) Meeting with Zekharya ha-Sofer: regarding copies and blank notebooks and exhorting him to fulfill prior instructions ("and let there be no third party other than God") and to bind the book that had been left with him. 
(3) Meeting with Dāʾūd al-Maghribī: informing him that the speaker has told Muḥammad to pay him the money, which he may take if he pleases. 
(4) Meeting with Abū l-Ḥasan al-Baghdādī: informing him that the kerchiefs have not yet sold but when they do he will receive the proceeds. 
(5) Meeting with Mufaḍḍal al-Zajjāj and asking him to collect the money from Zikrī, and then pay it to Muḥammad. 
(6) Mentions Shemuʾel the son of the brother of al-Rav; the rest is too damaged to read. 
(7) If Zikrī al-Maghribī has paid the money, then he should collect 29 qirṭās for Matitya and three selected notebooks. 
(8) Instructing Muḥammad not to neglect anything and to sell the rhubarb. 

Underneath there are jotted Judaeo-Arabic accounts in a different handwriting and ink. 

(Information in part from Goitein's attached notes.)
description_he: מסמך יוצא דופן המכיל פרוטוקולי פגישות עסקיות, ביהודית-ערבית, כנראה מהמאה ה-13 או ה-14. כולל פגישות עם זכריה הסופר, דאוד אל-מגרבי, אבו אל-חסן אל-בגדאדי ומופד'ל אל-זג'אג', בענייני ספרים, בדים ומכירות.
